• The move is part of India's broader strategy to reduce reliance on imports, achieve self-sufficiency in critical components, and boost its manufacturing sector under 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.
• Experts believe this PLI scheme will accelerate India's transition to green mobility and energy, creating jobs and fostering technological innovation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: New PLI scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing.
Aims to attract investments for domestic battery production.
Crucial for EVs and renewable energy storage.
Part of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
Offers incentives based on incremental sales.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and expected outcomes of the new PLI scheme for advanced battery manufacturing, particularly in the context of India's EV and renewable energy goals.
Discuss how the PLI scheme can contribute to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' by reducing import dependence and boosting domestic manufacturing capabilities in critical sectors.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global hub for ACC battery production, considering raw material availability and technological expertise.
Examine the broader economic impact of such incentive schemes on job creation, technological innovation, and sustainable industrial growth.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of an electric vehicle battery pack, glowing with energy.
GS3.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are currently the most common type of Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) batteries used in EVs and grid-scale energy storage.

GS3.headline: MoEFCC Releases Enhanced EPR Guidelines for Plastic Packaging to Tackle Pollution
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) issued updated and more stringent Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) guidelines for plastic packaging.
• The revised framework aims to streamline the collection, segregation, and recycling of plastic waste, holding producers, importers, and brand-owners accountable.
• Key changes include mandatory targets for recycling, increased use of recycled content in packaging, and a clearer mechanism for EPR certificate trading.
• The guidelines also introduce provisions for punitive action against non-compliance, emphasizing a shift towards a circular economy for plastics.
• This move is a critical step towards mitigating plastic pollution and managing the rapidly increasing volume of plastic waste generated in India.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
prelimsPoints: MoEFCC released enhanced EPR guidelines for plastic packaging.
Aims to streamline collection, segregation, recycling of plastic waste.
Mandatory recycling targets and increased use of recycled content.
Introduces EPR certificate trading and punitive actions for non-compliance.
Focus on circular economy for plastics.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the enhanced EPR guidelines for plastic packaging in addressing India's growing plastic waste crisis and promoting a circular economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by producers, importers, and brand-owners in complying with the new EPR targets and the role of various stakeholders in successful implementation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of mechanisms like EPR certificate trading and punitive actions in driving behavioral change and achieving waste management goals.
Examine the broader policy framework required to transition towards sustainable packaging solutions and reduce overall plastic consumption in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the lifecycle of plastic, from production to recycling, emphasizing a circular flow.
GS3.trivia: India generates approximately 3.5 million tonnes of plastic waste annually, with a significant portion remaining uncollected and unrecycled.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records All-Time High Growth, Fuels Economic Recovery
GS3.explainer:
• India's tourism sector has witnessed unprecedented growth in the past year, with both domestic and international tourist arrivals reaching record numbers.
• This surge is attributed to focused government initiatives, improved infrastructure, and aggressive marketing campaigns promoting diverse destinations.
• The growth has significantly contributed to economic recovery, generating substantial revenue and creating millions of jobs across various segments, including hospitality, transport, and local crafts.
• Special emphasis on spiritual tourism, eco-tourism, and adventure tourism has attracted new demographics of travelers.
• The Ministry of Tourism projects continued upward trajectory, positioning India as a global tourism powerhouse by the end of the decade.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the record growth in India's tourism sector and its role in post-pandemic economic recovery and job creation.
Discuss the various government policies and initiatives that have facilitated this growth, including infrastructure development and promotional campaigns.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Indian tourism industry in achieving sustainable growth, such as environmental impact and equitable distribution of benefits.
Suggest further measures to enhance India's competitiveness as a global tourist destination, focusing on niche tourism segments and digital marketing.
GS3.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian tourist destinations, including the Taj Mahal, Himalayan peaks, and Kerala backwaters.
GS3.trivia: The 'Incredible India' campaign is a prominent marketing initiative by the Indian government to promote tourism.

GS3.headline: Indian Researchers Develop Indigenous AI Model for Early Disease Detection in Rural Areas
GS3.explainer:
• A team of researchers from leading Indian institutions has developed an indigenous Artificial Intelligence (AI) model designed for early detection of common diseases.
• The model is specifically optimized for low-resource settings and aims to bridge healthcare gaps in rural and remote areas of India.
• It utilizes limited data inputs and can operate on basic smartphone devices, making it highly accessible and scalable.
• Initial trials show promising results in identifying prevalent conditions like tuberculosis, malnutrition, and certain non-communicable diseases with high accuracy.
• This breakthrough represents a significant step towards leveraging AI for inclusive healthcare delivery and improving public health outcomes in India.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Optimized for low-resource, rural settings.
Operates on basic smartphones with limited data.

mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of indigenous AI models, like the one developed for early disease detection, in transforming healthcare accessibility and outcomes in rural India.
Analyze the technical and logistical challenges in deploying AI-based healthcare solutions in low-resource settings and how they can be overcome.
Evaluate the ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with using AI for public health surveillance and individual diagnostics.
Examine the role of government policy and private-public partnerships in scaling up such technological innovations for national impact.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized image of a human brain with circuit patterns, overlaid with medical symbols, representing AI in healthcare.
GS3.trivia: AI in healthcare can analyze vast amounts of medical data, including patient records, research papers, and diagnostic images, to assist in diagnosis, treatment planning, and drug discovery.
